description:
Goal: make runtime and compiler builtins ordinary Compiler2 code contributions, with namespace bindings established by the same maps used for user source.

Change:
- Submit runtime/builtin packages through `CodeMap` and index them with ordinary `IndexCode`/module-surface jobs.
- Populate the initial namespace head from compiler builtin definitions and runtime module references, not from scheduler branches or lowerer prelude injection.
- Runtime definitions participate in the same `FunctionDefined`, dispatch-pure helper, entry-dispatch, and lowered-body facts as user code, and are lowered only when reachable semantic jobs ask for them.

Why:
- Compiler2 should not reproduce the old prelude path where lowering prepends runtime source and eagerly registers/lower bodies.
- Builtins are just early namespace facts plus ordinary code/function definitions; pull-through remains by function.

Checks:
- Resolving `Enum`, `Kernel`, and `Process` names uses namespace/module/function facts.
- Referencing a runtime function can wait on ordinary `CodeIndexed`, `ModuleDefined`, `FunctionDefined`, entry-dispatch, and `LoweredBody` facts.
- Unused runtime definitions do not produce lowered bodies, activations, executable facts, or artifact work.
title: Compiler2 runtime: index runtime libraries as ordinary code
